name: ecommerce
description: Structural bias for product-led pages optimized for scanning, comparison, and purchase decisions.
intent: Put product identity and key differentiators early, enable rapid comparison and filtering, and keep the purchase action visible throughout the decision journey.

sectionOrder.reason: Product pages must establish the item's identity, key image, and price context before details arrive.
Ecommerce decisions depend on variant selection, option comparison, and quantity or configuration choice.
Reviews, ratings, and usage evidence should follow product presentation, not precede understanding.
Return policy, shipping details, size guides, and FAQs should appear near the decision they affect.
Add-to-cart and checkout actions should persist throughout the product decision journey.

density.reason: Shoppers scan densely on category pages but need breathing room at the moment of commitment.

ctaCadence.secondaryActionRule: Secondary actions may surface wishlist, share, or lower-commitment paths as visually subordinate controls.
ctaCadence.reason: Purchase intent can arrive at any point in the scan; the primary action must be findable without a full read.

overlayUse.reason: Ecommerce overlays should support browsing efficiency without hiding required selection or comparison information.

antiPatterns.name: buried-purchase-action
antiPatterns.reason: The primary action must remain accessible throughout the product decision journey, not only at page end.

antiPatterns.name: proof-before-product-identity
antiPatterns.reason: Reviews placed before the visitor understands what the product is lose their persuasive value.

antiPatterns.name: hidden-price-or-variant
antiPatterns.reason: Requiring overlay or click-through to see price or variant options adds friction at the highest-value decision point.

antiPatterns.name: unfiltered-catalog-wall
antiPatterns.reason: Product grids without filter or sort surfaces force exhaustive scanning and block comparison decisions.
